0.q: Overexpression of PtrA/NINV conferred enhanced tolerance to multiple stresses...increased photosynthesis efficiency, relative to wild-type (WT).

1.q: The hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) accumulation in PtrMYB3 transgenic tobacco plants after salt stress was higher than the wild type (WT), further confirming that overexpression of PtrMYB3 inhibited PtrPOD-mediated H2O2 scavenging.

2.q: The accumulation of reactive oxygen species (ROS) in the transgenic line was appreciably decreased under the stresses, but ROS scavenging capacity was compromised when the transgenic plants were treated with the ADC inhibitor D-arginine prior to stress treatment.

3.q: Overexpression of PtrA/NINV conferred enhanced tolerance to multiple stresses, including cold, high salinity, and drought, as supported by lower levels of reactive oxygen species (ROS), reduced oxidative damages.

4.q: a peroxidase gene (PtrPOD) was found to be greatly upregulated in PtrMYB3-silenced trifoliate orange, and a dual LUC assay confirmed that PtrMYB3 could suppress the expression of PtrPOD

5.q: a dual LUC (luciferase) assay confirmed that PtrMYB3 could suppress the expression of PtrPOD

6.q: Overexpression of PtADC in an Arabidopsis thaliana ADC mutant adc1-1 promoted putrescine synthesis in the transgenic line.

7.q: overexpression of PtrMYB3 inhibited PtrPOD-mediated H2O2 scavenging

8.q: Overexpression of PtADC in an Arabidopsis thaliana ADC mutant adc1-1 promoted putrescine synthesis in the transgenic line

9.q: PtrA/NINV is an important gene implicated in sucrose decomposition, and plays a positive role in abiotic stress tolerance.
